examination_log.notes: Dr. C independent STT quality certification. WER: 100.0% (F). RTF: 0.0197 (real-time factor, <1 = faster than real-time). Mean latency: 191.4 ms. Peak GPU: 334 MB. Tested on FLEURS hindi dev split (15 clips). Filed by Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C) on 2026-04-12.

examination_log.method: STT WER expansion — 100 FLEURS clips (6.7× larger sample than baseline)
examination_log.notes: 100-sample FLEURS stress test (up from 15 samples). WER=0.999, CER=1.1335, RTF=0.0098, mean latency 104.9ms. WARNING: WER ≈100% confirms Hinglish-vs-Devanagari mismatch — model outputs romanized Hindi while FLEURS reference is Devanagari script. Product decision required. Filed by Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C).

examination_log.method: Product framing decision — Hinglish vs Devanagari output
examination_log.protocol_script: inline by Dr. C at Grant approval
examination_log.rationale: Base model (Oriserve/Whisper-Hindi2Hinglish-Swift) is explicitly designed to emit Latin-script Hinglish. The 99.9% FLEURS-Devanagari WER is a script mismatch, not a quality failure. Retraining a Devanagari-output variant would cost ~1 day of GPU on speculation of demand. Better to ship the current Hinglish model with crystal-clear README disclosure than to delay release on a hypothetical need. Revisit if customer feedback or benchmark coverage requests Devanagari.

examination_log.reversibility: Future Devanagari-output variant can ship as a separate repo (WindyWord/listen-windy-lingua-hindi-devanagari) without disturbing this one.
examination_log.notes: Grant greenlit option A (ship Hinglish with explicit labeling) on 2026-04-16. Filed by Opus 4.6 Opus-Claw (Dr. C).

consensus.notes: Shipping catalog entry from 2026-03-10. Local merged weights not present — can only be tested after pulling from HuggingFace or remote machine.

hf_upload_notes.important_note: This model transcribes **Hindi speech into romanized (Hinglish) text**, not Devanagari. Output is suitable for chat, social-media captions, code-switched messaging, and any downstream use expecting Latin-script Hindi. If you need Devanagari output, this is not the right model. A Devanagari-output variant may be added as a separate repo in future (not yet available).
hf_upload_notes.base_model_rationale: Base model is Oriserve/Whisper-Hindi2Hinglish-Swift, explicitly designed to emit Hinglish output. The Hinglish behaviour is by design, not a defect.
hf_upload_notes.benchmark_disclosure: FLEURS Hindi dev set (Devanagari reference) measures this model at ~100% WER. This is a script mismatch, not a quality signal: the model correctly transcribes audio but in Latin characters while the reference is Devanagari. A Hinglish-script WER benchmark is not yet established in the public STT ecosystem. Qualitative inspection of transcripts (see patient file examination_log) shows the model produces coherent, grammatically reasonable Hinglish of the spoken Hindi audio.

quality_rating.fleurs_hi_devanagari_wer_note: Script mismatch — not a quality signal. FLEURS reference is Devanagari; model outputs Latin-script Hinglish by design.
quality_rating.qualitative_grade: B (Hinglish transcripts coherent; quantitative Hinglish-reference benchmark pending)
production_ready_for: Hindi voice input → Latin-script output pipelines
not_production_ready_for: Applications expecting Devanagari output (requires separate Devanagari-output model)
